It芒聙聶s Day 14 of the Olympics. Just one more Friday and then the final two days fall over the weekend. There are tons of medals still left to be handed out, and once again I芒聙聶m back with my daily watch guide to help you determine where to set your eyes.

It芒聙聶s also a great time to subscribe toSports Illustrated芒聙聶s Daily Rings, my daily podcast where Dan Gartland and I recap every sport every day. Join now so you芒聙聶ll be with us in time to put a bow on the entire Olympics next when they are over.脗

Here芒聙聶s what I芒聙聶ll be watching Friday, August 9.

This is not a comprehensive list;visit Olympics.comfor a full schedule. All events will stream live on Peacock. I芒聙聶ll also be tweeting out a schedule of medal events, Team USA games and other favorites each day.

My Day 14 Olympics viewing guide! It's Friday, and there are *A TON* of medals being handed out#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/igS6FFi0L5

It has been much discussed over the past few years, but breaking will finally make its Olympic debut over the next two days. Yes, it芒聙聶s breakdancing. Unlike gymnastics, they will have to freestyle with no control over what music is played.

You may be expecting young athletes, but the U.S. women芒聙聶s team (they actually call them B-Girls) is led by 35-year-old Sunni Choi, who won a silver at the 2022 World Games.

A round robin starts at 10 a.m. ET, and the finals start shortly after 3 p.m. ET. The B-Boys will compete Saturday. It芒聙聶s going to be interesting, and people are going to be talking about it, so you might as well check it out for yourself to see what all the fuss is about.

Friday芒聙聶s schedule has two big games with medals on the line. The women芒聙聶s bronze game is between Spain and Germany at 9 a.m. ET.脗

Then we芒聙聶ll see the gold medal game to wrap up the men芒聙聶s competition at noon ET. It芒聙聶ll be the host country, France, against Spain. Spain won silver in Tokyo, but France has not won a medal in men芒聙聶s soccer since taking gold back in 1984.

And finally, we have just two days left of beach volleyball. On Friday they芒聙聶ll play the women芒聙聶s bronze and gold medal matches, followed the next day by the men芒聙聶s bronze and gold matches.

All four of Team USA芒聙聶s pairs were knocked out before the medal rounds, but it芒聙聶ll still be great to watch the conclusion of what has been a fun tournament in an iconic location right at the foot of the Eiffel Tower.脗

Teams from Switzerland and Australia will battle for bronze at 3 p.m. ET, followed by the gold medal match between teams from Canada and Brazil at 4:30.

Mitch Goldich is a senior editor for Sports Illustrated, mostly focused on the NFL. He has also covered the Olympics extensively and written on a variety of sports since joining SI in 2014. His work has been published by The New York Times, Baseball Prospectus and Food & Wine, among other outlets. Goldich has a bachelor芒聙聶s in journalism from Lehigh University and a master芒聙聶s in journalism from the Medill School at Northwestern University. He is a Philly native, now living in Boston, and he invented and popularized the octopus, when the same player scores a touchdown and the ensuing two-point conversion.
